true_header: PCH Median Improvements Project ¾ Updates:  The project was approved by the Planning Commission on September 8, 2021. Outdoor Warning Sirens - Design (FEMA Project) ¾ Project Description:  An Emergency Warning Siren system will improve the City’s  ability  to  alert  the  community  by  providing  mechanisms  to  alert residents in areas without power and those who live in neighborhoods with poor  cell  service.    Phase  One  of  the  project  included  a  sound  study  to determine the optimal number and locations of individual sirens, power and infrastructure  requirements,  and  siren  sound  range.    The  City  has  been awarded a FEMA Hazard Mitigation grant to fund the design, engineering and environmental compliance needed for a shovel ready project.  Phase Two of the Emergency Warning Sirens project will be implementation of the design plan,  including  purchasing,  installing,  and  testing  the  sirens.    The  City  will apply for future grant funding for this phase of the project.
